minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.abstract: In an effort to improve short-sea shipping in Europe, we present a 3D world interpreter (3DWI) system as part of a robotic container-handling system. The 3DWI is an advanced sensor suite combined with AI-based software and the communication infrastructure to connect to both the crane control and the shore control center. On input of LiDAR data and stereo captures, the 3DWI builds a world model of the operating environment and detects containers. The 3DWI and crane control are the core of an auto

minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.title: Interaction-Aware Model Predictive Decision-Making for Socially-Compliant Autonomous Driving in Mixed Urban Traffic Scenarios
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.venue: arXiv.org
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.abstract: Autonomous vehicles must negotiate with pedestrians in ways that are both safe and socially compliant. We present an interaction-aware model predictive decision-making (IAMPDM) framework that integrates a gap-acceptance-inspired intention model with MPC to jointly reason about human intent and vehicle control in real time. The pedestrian module produces a continuous crossing-propensity signal - driven by time-to-collision (TTC) with an intention discounting mechanism - that modulates MPC safety

minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.title: Safe Motion Planning for Multi-Vehicle Autonomous Driving in Uncertain Environment
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.venue: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.abstract: In the field of motion planning for autonomous driving systems, ensuring the safety of multi-vehicle navigation is one of the crucial topics. An unavoidable problem in practice is that the noise-induced uncertainties in real-world applications highly degrade the safety of multi-vehicle navigation. It is also challenging to guarantee the required computation efficiency of motion planning algorithms in such uncertain environments. In this work, we present a novel motion planning framework to enhan

minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.title: Counterfactual regret minimization for the safety verification of autonomous driving
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.venue: Applied intelligence (Boston)

minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.title: Radar Perception of Multi-Object Collision Risk Neural Domains during Autonomous Driving
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.venue: Electronics
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.abstract: The analysis of the state of the literature in the field of methods of perception and control of the movement of autonomous vehicles shows the possibilities of improving them by using an artificial neural network to generate domains of prohibited maneuvers of passing objects, contributing to increasing the safety of autonomous driving in various real conditions of the surrounding environment. This article concerns radar perception, which involves receiving information about the movement of many

minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.title: Moral Testing of Autonomous Driving Systems
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.venue: 2025 IEEE/ACM 1st International Workshop on Software Engineering for Autonomous Driving Systems (SE4ADS)
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.abstract: Autonomous Driving System (ADS) testing plays a crucial role in their development, with the current focus primarily on functional and safety testing. However, evaluating the non-functional morality of ADSs, particularly their decision-making capabilities in unavoidable collision scenarios, is equally important to ensure the systems’ trustworthiness and public acceptance. Unfortunately, testing ADS morality is nearly impossible due to the absence of universal moral principles. To address this cha

minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.title: RADAR-Based Safe Pull-Over of Autonomous Racing Cars in Localization Failure Scenarios
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.venue: European Control Conference
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.abstract: This paper presents a RADAR-Based, vehicle lateral dynamics control algorithm that ensures a safe pull-over of autonomous racing vehicles to the roadside barriers in the event of localization failures. The position and curvature of the roadside barriers are estimated from RADAR measurements through a Total Least Squares algorithm and fed to a Linear-Quadratic-Regulator (LQR), which outputs the steering commands to the vehicle. As the estimates accuracy varies with the distance to the barriers, t

minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.title: Research on emergency collision avoidance planning for autonomous vehicles
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.venue: Proceedings of the Institution of mechanical engineers. Part D, journal of automobile engineering
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.abstract: A safe and reliable collision avoidance strategy is deemed crucial for autonomous driving. Pedestrians crossing the road illegally present a high-risk collision scenario, which can easily lead to emergency collision avoidance situations for autonomous vehicles, potentially resulting in unavoidable collisions. Therefore, developing an emergency collision avoidance strategy that combines efficient computation and minimal collision cost is of paramount importance. In this paper, a collision avoidan

minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.title: Continuous Decision-Making for Risk-Based Collision Avoidance in Autonomous Driving Using Twin-Delayed DDPG
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.venue: International Conference on Industrial Informatics
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.abstract: The issue of collision avoidance (CA) represents a core challenge for the safe operation of autonomous driving vehicles. In this paper, we propose a novel CA method that integrates a risk evaluation strategy into deep reinforcement learning (DRL). The method formulates the CA problem in autonomous driving as a minimum risk optimization problem (MROP). To solve this formulated MROP, a risk evaluation model was first constructed to estimate the specific risk probabilities of a given driving state.

minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.title: Minimum decision of collision injury based on drivable area in unavoidable scene
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.venue: Proceedings of the Institution of mechanical engineers. Part D, journal of automobile engineering
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.abstract: The existing technology for assisted driving collision avoidance has improved the active safety of vehicles, but it is primarily designed for scenarios where collisions can be avoided. It is not suitable for situations where pedestrians suddenly intrude. Therefore, there is a need to develop a control strategy that can minimize collision injuries in unavoidable situations. This study focuses on typical collision scenarios where pedestrians suddenly intrude. It proposes a real-time online double-

minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.title: A Necessary Criterion for Evaluating Scene-Level Criticality Metrics in Safety Verification of Autonomous Driving
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.venue: 2025 IEEE Intelligent Vehicles Symposium (IV)
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.abstract: Effective, reliable, and efficient measurement of autonomous driving safety performance is essential for demonstrating its trustworthiness. Criticality metrics offer an objective assessment of autonomous driving safety. However, the wide variety of criticality metrics, each with distinct characteristics, lacks a unified standard for evaluation and selection. We contend that a criticality metric should accurately reflect the true danger level of vehicle pairs at risk. This paper focuses on scene-

minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.title: AuthSim: Toward Authentic and Effective Safety-Critical Scenario Generation for Autonomous Driving Tests
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.venue: IEEE transactions on intelligent transportation systems (Print)
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.abstract: The generation of adversarial safety-critical scenarios is essential for rigorously evaluating autonomous driving systems, enabling the identification of vulnerabilities and enhancement of system robustness. However, existing methodologies predominantly focus on extreme, unconstrained collision scenarios in which non-player character (NPC) vehicles exhibit unrealistic adversarial behaviors toward the ego vehicle. While such scenarios serve as stress tests, their practical utility is limited due

minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.title: Diffusion-Based Corner Scenario Generation Method for Autonomous Driving with a Dynamics-Based Decoder
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.venue: 2025 9th CAA International Conference on Vehicular Control and Intelligence (CVCI)
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.abstract: Ensuring the safety of autonomous driving systems requires the generation of high-risk traffic scenarios. However, existing methods often lack physical plausibility, diversity, and controllability. This paper proposes a logical corner scenario generation framework integrating rule extraction, a risk-aware conditional diffusion model, and a dynamics-based decoder. Cut-in scenarios with annotated risk levels are extracted from naturalistic data using rules based on minimum time to collision (TTC)

minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.title: QLSL: A Symbolic Logic Enhanced Q-Learning Approach for Safe Autonomous Driving
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.venue: 2025 International Conference on Cyber-Physical Social Intelligence (CPSI)
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.abstract: Safe autonomous driving faces a fundamental challenge: reinforcement learning requires exploration that may compromise safety, while purely symbolic methods lack environmental adaptability. This paper presents Q-Learning with Symbolic Logic (QLSL), a neuro-symbolic framework that integrates first-order logic constraints with Q-Learning to achieve hard safety guarantees. QLSL employs a two-stage decision architecture where symbolic logic filtering first generates permissible actions based on safe

minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.title: The Safety Verification of Autonomous Driving on Nested Game Algorithm
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.venue: 2025 2nd International Conference on Machine Learning, Pattern Recognition and Automation Engineering (MLPRAE)
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.abstract: In the testing and evaluation of autonomous vehicles, the low incidence of safety accidents remains a key challenge. To address this issue, this paper proposes a nested game model that utilises the Counterfactual Regret Minimisation (CFR) algorithm to validate the safety performance of autonomous vehicles in an acceleration test environment. In the experimental design, a driving risk field model is introduced as a metric for scene generation, with time to collision (TTC) and minimum safe distanc

minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.title: Learning Neural Control Barrier Functions from Expert Demonstrations using Inverse Constraint Learning
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.venue: arXiv.org
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.abstract: Safety is a fundamental requirement for autonomous systems operating in critical domains. Control barrier functions (CBFs) have been used to design safety filters that minimally alter nominal controls for such systems to maintain their safety. Learning neural CBFs has been proposed as a data-driven alternative for their computationally expensive optimization-based synthesis. However, it is often the case that the failure set of states that should be avoided is non-obvious or hard to specify form

minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.title: Blockchain for secure Data Sharing: Enhancing Security and Trust in Inter-organizational Exchange
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.venue: Journal of Information Systems Engineering & Management
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.abstract: Blockchain technology has changed the way people share secure data by providing an autonomous, unchangeable, and cryptographically secure structure. Instead of traditional controlled systems that have trust problems and single points of failure, blockchain allows for a distributed ledger system where everyone shares confirmed, unchangeable records without the need for middlemen. Its immutability protects the purity of the data, and its openness encourages cooperation without trust, which lowers

minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.title: Safe and Non-Conservative Contingency Planning for Autonomous Vehicles via Online Learning-Based Reachable Set Barriers
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.venue: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
minimum_harm_unavoidable_collision.abstract: Autonomous vehicles must navigate dynamically uncertain environments while balancing safety and efficiency. This challenge is exacerbated by unpredictable human-driven vehicle (HV) behaviors and perception inaccuracies, necessitating planners that adapt to evolving uncertainties while maintaining safe trajectories. Overly conservative planning degrades driving efficiency, while deterministic methods risk failure in unexpected scenarios. To address these issues, we propose a real-time contingency
